confirm_mermas_desc: Negative differences have been detected in inventory. Please specify the reason for each loss for cost control.
history_subtitle: Permanent waste and loss history. Filter by month and year with the selectors.
btn_mermas_info_tooltip: How waste is recorded, how long it's kept, and how it affects food cost
btn_tutorial_video_tooltip: Video tutorial: how to use the Inventory tab
info_mermas_lead: Everything you need to know about how waste is generated, where to see it, and how it affects your costs.
info_mermas_manual_body: Click "Quick Waste" to log a one-off loss: expiration, breakage, theft, kitchen error, etc. Specify ingredient, quantity, reason and optional note. Stock drops immediately.
info_mermas_auto_body: When uploading a physical inventory from Excel, detected differences (real stock lower than system stock) are recorded as waste with reason "Inventory adjustment" and a note with the upload date. If real stock is higher (unrecorded incoming goods), it's treated as an upward adjustment, NOT waste.
info_mermas_value_body: Each waste entry is valued in euros as quantity × average purchase price of the ingredient. With no purchase history, it falls back to the configured price or the per-unit format price. The amount you see in the history is the real cost of what was lost.
info_mermas_history_body: Every waste record is kept indefinitely — never automatically deleted, not even at month-end. Filter by month/year using the selectors in "View Waste History". Daily backup to MinIO S3.
info_mermas_impact_body: The traditional "Food cost" (in dashboard and other tabs) measures only what was sold: sales COGS / revenue. But money THROWN AWAY through waste is also a real cost — it just doesn't show up as a "sale". In the monthly executive report from Chat AI you'll see TWO numbers: "Sales food cost" (the classic one) and "Real food cost" (including waste). If the gap between them is large, you have an operational problem: you're buying food that never makes it onto a plate.
info_mermas_tip_body: Run a physical inventory at least once a month (month-end/start is easiest). Waste detected during upload is recorded and helps you understand where your product goes. Without periodic physical inventory, system stock drifts from reality over time.
format_configure_hint: Configure purchase format to use conversion

massive_columns_hint: Upload an Excel (.xlsx) or CSV file with the columns: Ingredient and Real Stock
